video_title: Seriously Over-the-Top Stuffed Pastas on #DDD with Guy Fieri 🍝  Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives

restaurants.name: Little Donkey
restaurants.guy_intro: We're in Central Square here in Cambridge. You got MIT down the road. You got Harvard over here. I went to both. I was just on a tour, but I went there. Anyhow, where are we going? So we're going to see buddy Ken Oranger, who's amazing. I love this guy. This is Little Donkey.

dishes.description: Street food from Istanbul, meat and lamb raviolis with cream, heat, and flavor. Stuffed with beef, Aleppo oil, grated onions, parsley, ground cumin, ground coriander, salt, black pepper. Served with garlic yogurt and Aleppo butter.
dishes.guy_response: I love the texture of the dough. Like a real ravioli, but thicker. I like that it's not that delicate. It's a peasant dish. I love the particulate that you get from the Aleppo in the butter. But the real kicker on this is that garlic yogurt. The creaminess and the cold to the warm and the hot. Wow, so good.

restaurants.name: Louie's Family Restaurant
restaurants.guy_intro: Here I am, Providence, Rhode Island, at a family-owned restaurant that's been open for more than 62 years. Now, a lot of businesses have come and gone in this area, but not this joint. They're still serving great food at reasonable prices to college students, because that's what Louie's family restaurant is all about.

dishes.description: Handmade ravioli stuffed with shredded barbecue chicken, cheese, spinach, and soup. Served with marinara sauce. Barbecue sauce made with tomato sauce, onions, honey, vinegar, orange juice, garlic, paprika, red chili flake, coriander, sage, oregano, pepper, salt, rosemary, parsley.
dishes.guy_response: That right there is as good as any ravioli you will find off the hook. Ooh, the spinach, the cheese, stuffed full. That's just awesome. Made the barbecue sauce, barbecued the chicken, made the marinara, and handmade the ravioli.

restaurants.name: Antonino's Taverna
restaurants.guy_intro: So I'm here on the south side of St. Louis, Missouri, at one of the highest points of the city, known as The Hill. Now, this area is loaded with great Italian restaurants and specialty stores. So I'm here to check out a little joint that's Italian ... You'd think I'm going to say Italian-American. That's what you're thinking, Italian-American. But no, it's something we don't see on Triple D that often. It's Italian-Greek, owned by two brothers. This is Antonino's Taverna.

dishes.description: St. Louis finger food. Raviolis filled with ground beef, ricotta, pecorino romano, black pepper, salt, onion, granulated garlic, made into a smooth, fluffy paste. Fried until crispy, served with marinara sauce and fresh romano cheese.
dishes.guy_response: Got a nice bite to it. A lot of flavor. Mm. What I like about the marinara, a San Marzano, a ton of basil, a little bit of sweetness. It works together with the ravioli incredibly well. Look at the amount of filling that goes inside of this. Good, old school. Mm, sick. Oh, blah, blah, blah. I said that's ridiculous.

restaurants.name: Redwood
restaurants.guy_intro: So I'm here in Minneapolis, Minnesota. Now, we get recommendations for Triple D joints from a lot of people. But in particular, when your crew that shoots Triple D, hold on, this crew right here, when they tell you that you got to come check out a pizza joint that's right down the street from their house, well, you know I'm there. This is Redwood.

dishes.description: 14-inch pizza with housemade sauce, half mozzarella, half provolone, house sausage, banana peppers, sopressata ribbon, buzzed crushed red pepper, house-made garlic sauce, fig-infused balsamic. Dough made with day-old dough (mother), cold water, dry active yeast, heritage wheat flour, salt. Sausage made with pork butt, bacon, sopressata, garlic, onion, fennel powder, salt, ground pepper.
dishes.guy_response: It's dynamite. Everything you're doing in the pizza is balanced. Fresh-made sausage. You've got handmade dough. You've got homemade sauce. You got a nice little acid bite in there coming from the banana pepper, a little touch of the chili flake. It's an artisan pizza, man. Everybody at home right now is going, oh, I want a bite of that pizza, because it's the real deal. Fantastic.

dishes.description: Homemade tortellini filled with spinach puree (ice, blanched spinach, sauteed garlic, onion, salt, pepper, oil) and housemade ricotta (whole milk, heavy cream, lemon juice, apple cider vinegar). Served with white wine, cheesy bechamel, ham, fresh peas, and gremolata (Parmesan, red pepper flake, oregano).
dishes.guy_response: It's fantastic. We play full speed. You only have a 10. Like the knob got broken. A 10. Outstanding. I would not expect a tortellini that good to be found at a pizza place. It's the place you have to go. You just have to try it. Phenomenal.

restaurants.name: Little Napoli
restaurants.guy_intro: So I'm here in this cool little town called Carmel-by-the-Sea, California. Great restaurants. Lots of cool little shops. And I'm hanging out with my buddy here, G Garvin, from the Cooking Channel. Now, G, I know what you want. You're saying, get me some really good barbecue, some sweet potatoes. No barbecue. Then how about Italian? This is Little Napoli.

dishes.description: Cannelloni with red, white, and green sauces like the Italian flag. Stuffed with grilled chicken, onions, zucchini, yellow squash, mushrooms, parsley. Served with marinara, pesto cream sauce, and alfredo sauce. Topped with shaved Parmigiana, roasted pine nuts. Pasta is traditional semolina and egg.
dishes.guy_response: Get out. Three words. Forget about it. Ooh. That's the best compliment you can get. To me, one of the keys to this that I'm really digging is the simplicity. You execute the pasta perfectly. You make your own sauces. But what makes the dish so great is with so many flavors on the plate, there's no reason to make it any more complex. Just let the simplicity of it shine through. And that's really what I enjoy the most. Man, it all works well together. It's phenomenal.

restaurants.name: Pineville Tavern
restaurants.guy_intro: So here I am in the center of Bucks County, PA. Now for everyone from Pennsylvania, New York, and New Jersey, you know where that is. But for a guy from California, I'll just say I'm 30 miles north of Philadelphia to check out a joint in a building from 1742 and to try some of their snapping turtle soup. This is Pineville Tavern.

dishes.description: Ravioli filled with a mixture of beef, veal, pork, minced shallot, minced garlic, salt, black pepper, granulated garlic, ricotta, hard skim mozzarella, grated Locatelli Romano, chopped flat-leaf parsley, eggs. Served with a basic marinara sauce (olive oil, garlic, San Marzano tomatoes) and topped with fresh parsley and grated Locatelli Romano. Pasta is flour and eggs.
dishes.guy_response: Mm. That is all thriller, no filler. You got the balance. Exact amount of creaminess. You taste the texture of the ricotta still. Then you get the texture of the meat. And the flavor's right on point with it. The marinara adds that acid. A little bit of that sting from some of that cheese right on top of it.

restaurants.name: Catelli's
restaurants.guy_intro: I'm here in a cool little town called Geyserville in the Northern California wine country to check out a place that I kind of have some history with. I imagine a coach feels really proud when one of his players turns pro, because that's how I feel when one of my guys that's worked with me in my restaurants ventures out to open his own place. That brings us here to Catelli's.

dishes.description: Ravioli filling made with ground pork butt, beef chunks, chicken (skin on), house-made sausage, onions, celery, garlic, Swiss chard, bread, pecorino, parmesan, thyme, basil, Italian parsley, rosemary, sage, eggs. Pasta dough made with eggs and super fine "topia zero" flour. Served with butter, garlic, salt, parsley, and Parmesan cheese.
dishes.guy_response: Oh, so tender, delicate, and so rich. Small, dainty, handmade perfection is what this is. This is one of the best raviolis I've ever had.

restaurants.name: Reno's Place
restaurants.guy_intro: You know I love Italian food. It's gotta be handmade. It's gotta be homemade. It's gotta be the real deal. And from what the locals tell me, Reno's place is all that.

dishes.description: Ravioli filled with boiled lobster meat (tail, claw, legs), sauteed shallots, fresh parsley, salt, pepper, fresh ricotta (from Italy), Parmigiano-Reggiano. Pasta dough made with eggs, salt, extra virgin olive oil, flour. Served with a sauce made from extra virgin olive oil, shallots, lobster meat, brandy, salt, pepper, and Parmigiano-Reggiano.
dishes.guy_response: I can feel the firmness of the pasta. Mmm. Wow. That's ridiculous. I don't know if I should hug you or kidnap you to California.
